snippet.title: ViddyScribe - Gemini powered accessibility for Video
snippet.description:
How does the Blind and low-vision community enjoy their own video content? This was the problem that the two-person team at ViddyScribe wanted to solve with AI-generated audio descriptions. Using Google's Gemini Flash, ViddyScribe quickly generates full audio descriptions of any video with a simple drag-and-drop interface. Learn how the creators found their inspiration and made it a reality with Gemini Flash. 

Resources:
See how this video sounds with audio-description → https://youtu.be/Kyhd1jbD7zk
Start building with Gemini API → https://goo.gle/3Qsa5Fs 
Learn more about the Hull Foundation and Learning Center →  https://goo.gle/3R9iHRS 

Subscribe to Google AI Developers → https://goo.gle/ai-for-devs

snippet.title: Waymo: AI in the physical world powering the future of driving
snippet.description:
Hear from Dmitri Dolgov, Co-CEO of Waymo — a driving force in the autonomous vehicle industry dedicated to building the world's most trusted driver. Waymo’s vehicles are the most advanced application of AI in the physical world, now serving over 250K weekly rides. As a pioneer in this transformative technology, Dmitri will take us on a ride through the history of autonomous driving, the critical role of AI in this tech’s development and deployment, and the exciting possibilities that lie ahead.

snippet.title: Quantum computing: reading signals from the noise
snippet.description:
There's been a lot of talk about quantum computing lately. But for the developers and scientists among us, how can we detect the signal from the noise? In this fireside chat we’ll get into the big and interesting questions on this often misunderstood technology; from how to assess true breakthroughs, to applications, encryption concerns, and more—let’s get into some real talk on quantum computing!

Resources:

snippet.title: Turing winner David Patterson: how to give AI a bad carbon footprint
snippet.description:
Join Turing Award laureate David Patterson for a look at the environmental considerations of AI. David will give tongue-in-cheek advice on how to make AI’s carbon footprint worse, and then how to make it better. He will dispel common fallacies about AI’s emissions. Learn key factors influencing AI's carbon footprint and gain valuable perspectives on building more sustainable AI systems.

Resources: 
The Carbon Footprint of Machine Learning Training Will Plateau, Then Shrink → https://goo.gle/3GMakKa 
Energy and Emissions of Machine Learning on Smartphones versus the Cloud: A Google Case Study → https://goo.gle/4k9Nlab 
Energy Is Physics, but Emissions Is Accounting: What’s Really Green? → https://goo.gle/459Tpeq 
Life-Cycle Emissions of AI Hardware: A Cradle-To-Grave Approach and Generational Trends → https://goo.gle/44vWPIo

snippet.title: What's new in Go
snippet.description:
There’s a lot to love about Go 1.24, including support for post-quantum cryptography, full support for generic type aliases, and several performance improvements to the Go runtime that significantly decrease CPU overhead for most applications. Learn what’s new, what’s ahead, and how you can use Go to build scalable, secure, and reliable applications — including for gen AI workloads.

snippet.title: Train your JAX models using model.fit(...) in Keras 3
snippet.description:
The Keras ecosystem offers many on-ramps to JAX. Use our classic and well-loved API patterns such as `model.fit(...)` to author and train models using JAX. Want to start with a pre-trained model? Try KerasHub, our catalog of 100+ model architectures with support for thousands of HuggingFace checkpoints. Fine-tune and optimize them using LoRA and qLoRA with JAX (or your choice of backend). We’ll also introduce Keras Recommenders, the latest addition to the Keras ecosystem.

snippet.title: What is JAX?
snippet.description:
JAX is a Python library for high-performance machine learning research. It combines the familiarity of NumPy with hardware acceleration and composable function transformations. Google researchers have built and trained models like Gemini and Gemma on JAX, and it’s also used by researchers for a wide range of advanced applications. This talk will provide an introduction to JAX and the Flax neural network library, showcasing recent features and how to get started.

snippet.title: Build a Transformer with JAX
snippet.description:
General purpose transformer architecture has really "transformed" the AI landscape. Learn about its origins and structure, and see it built from scratch! We’ll walk through building a small transformer on JAX, using Flax NNX to build the model architecture, Optax for loss function and optimizer creation, and training on accelerated hardware with the help of Orbax and XLA. Get a taste of development on JAX, and prepare to take your own next steps in building and training AI models.

snippet.title: Google’s DolphinGemma: One Giant Splash Towards Interspecies Communication
snippet.description:
DolphinGemma is the world’s first large language model for dolphins. This groundbreaking model has been fine-tuned on 40 years of field research collected by Dr. Denise Herzing, Director of Research at the Wild Dolphin Project. Denise and DeepMind researcher Thad Starner are working towards their goal of building a shared vocabulary between humans and dolphins.
The best part? DolphinGemma is open-model, so anyone can build with it — researchers, developers, and dolphin enthusiasts alike.
#GoogleIO 

Subscribe to Google for Developers → https://goo.gle/developers

snippet.title: Gemma | Chess: Learn, analyze, and discover a new dimension
snippet.description:
Ready to take your chess game to the next level? In this video, we explore the incredible potential of Gemma, a powerful AI, to revolutionize how you interact with chess!

Discover how Gemma can make learning the intricacies of chess easier and more intuitive. We'll show you how it can help you analyze your games with human-like insights, going beyond traditional engine evaluations. Plus, get ready to discover a whole new dimension of chess as Gemma helps bring the game to life through fascinating stories about matches.

Whether you're a beginner looking to grasp the fundamentals or an experienced player seeking a fresh perspective, Gemma offers exciting new ways to engage with the world of chess.

4:47 - Recap

snippet.title: Build a Multimodal Live Streaming Agent with ADK
snippet.description:
Want to build AI agents that can see, hear, and react in real-time? 🤔 We kick things off by diving deep into the challenging process of developing live agents directly with Gemini models' Live Streaming API, showing you what it takes to build complex function calling management from the ground up. But what if there was a much simpler way? Then, discover how the Agent Development Kit (ADK) revolutionizes this, drastically simplifying the creation of powerful live agents by expertly handling core components like live queue requests and event management. ✨ Start building! Watch our full video to learn how. 

Chapters: 
0:00  - Intro
1:09 - Live Agent Architecture - Gemini API
3:38  - Code - Gemini Live API
5:04 - Demo - Gemini Live API
6:45 - Live Agent Architecture - ADK
8:44 - Code - ADK
15:49 - Demo - ADK
18:22 - Recap and resources
20:24 - Outro

Resources: 
Access the ADK streaming documentation → https://google.github.io/adk-docs/streaming  
Clone and use our code (Live Streaming API) → https://goo.gle/4mcyf5J
Clone and use our code (Live Agents with A2A& MCP)→https://goo.gle/4mlpxSG 

Subscribe to Google for Developers → https://goo.gle/developers

Products Mentioned: Agent Developer Kit (ADK)

snippet.title: Build a Browser Use Agent with ADK and Selenium
snippet.description:
Elevate your retail website's performance with a Browser Use agent, built with Agent Development Kit and Selenium, to enhance product data. This powerful tool generates relevant keywords from your product information, then actively visits your site, analyzes top search results, and provides actionable suggestions to enrich your product titles for better visibility. By identifying and filling gaps in your product data, the agent helps ensure your website users always find products by your brand. 

Recources:

Get Started → https://goo.gle/3RGrB9T
